title: Herrania purpurea
taxonomy: Herrania purpurea is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is part of the order Gentianales. Herrania purpurea is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows as a tree or shrub. The plant's classification is further broken down into the genus Herrania and the species purpurea. Herrania purpurea is a member of the plant kingdom, specifically within the angiosperms.
uses: Herrania purpurea, a plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes.
